description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped natural rock with a rough, weathered surface and a predominantly gray coloration, interspersed with patches of white and subtle hints of mossy green. The rock is composed of two main masses, one taller and more upright, the other lower and more horizontally oriented, separated by a visible crevice. The surface texture is coarse and uneven, with jagged edges, small protrusions, and occasional flat areas. At the base, tufts of grass and earth are visible, suggesting the rock is partially embedded in the ground. The rock's coloration and texture indicate exposure to the elements, with some areas showing signs of lichen or moss growth. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural barrier or landscape feature in an urban park or green space.
description: A large, rough, gray rock with jagged edges and patches of moss, partially embedded in grassy earth. It consists of two main masses separated by a crevice, giving it a rugged, natural appearance.
description_view_0: This view highlights the taller, upright mass of the rock with a prominent vertical face and visible rough texture.
description_view_1: The rock appears as two distinct masses with a clear crevice between them, showing more of the horizontal spread and grassy base.
description_view_2: The elongated side of the rock is visible, emphasizing its slanted profile and the uneven, jagged surface.
description_view_3: This angle shows the lower, broader mass in the foreground and a smaller detached stone nearby, with grass surrounding the base.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped green space
